visa_policy.notes: Source is the official Barbados Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Foreign Trade visa list, revised May 9, 2025. Countries requiring a visa to enter Barbados (not listed above) include: Afghanistan, Bosnia Herzegovina, Dominican Republic, Eritrea, Iran, Iraq, Korea (North), Kosovo, Laos, Libya, Myanmar, Papua New Guinea, Somalia, Syria, Taiwan, Yemen. Haiti requires a visa for regular passport holders but not for Diplomatic and Official passport holders. Entry visa process is fully online via apps.immigration.gov.bb. Single entry visa fee USD$107; Multiple entry visa fee USD$211. ALL visitors (including visa-free nationals) are encouraged to complete an online Immigration and Customs Form at travelform.gov.bb, available 72 hours before arrival. The form is free and may also be completed at a kiosk on arrival — it is NOT mandatory to complete before departure. This is an administrative entry processing form - not a visa, ETA, or travel authorization - and does not change the visa_policy classification for any nationality.

cbi.notes: Barbados does not have a formal Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is obtained through birth, descent, marriage, or naturalization after a qualifying period of residency. No official government source advertises a direct CBI program.

programs.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) - Category 1
programs.notes: Requires investment of at least USD $2 million from funds sourced outside Barbados, plus proof of net worth exceeding USD $5 million. Category 1 holders are entitled to a work permit. The official immigration.gov.bb page states an application fee of BDS $300; secondary sources cite USD $3,500 (applicants under 60) and USD $5,000 (applicants over 60) - the higher figures could not be confirmed from the official government page directly.

programs.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) - Category 2
programs.notes: Requires ownership of property in Barbados valued at minimum USD $300,000. Renewable 5-year permit. Category 2 holders are not eligible for work permits. Official immigration.gov.bb page shows application fee of BDS $300.

programs.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) - Category 3
programs.notes: For persons who possess special skills deemed critical to the growth and development of Barbados. No investment threshold.

programs.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) - Category 4
programs.notes: For parents or grandparents of a citizen of Barbados who are over the age of 60 years. No investment threshold.

programs.name: Barbados Welcome Stamp
programs.eligibility: Remote workers, freelancers, and entrepreneurs who work location-independently for companies or individuals outside Barbados. Minimum annual income of USD $50,000. Individuals and families qualify. Participants may not accept employment from or offer services to persons or entities carrying on business in Barbados.
programs.notes: Program introduced by the Barbados Government on June 30, 2020. Cabinet approved renewal of the Welcome Stamp Visa until December 31, 2026 at its meeting of October 26, 2023. Valid for 12 months; renewable. Individual fee: USD $2,000; Family fee: USD $3,000 (non-refundable, due within 28 days of approval). Welcome Stamp holders are not subject to Barbados income tax for the 12-month period (17.5% VAT on goods/services still applies). Application portal: barbadoswelcomestamp.bb/applynow (Form C-5 under the Immigration Act). Official government page confirmed at foreign.gov.bb.

conditional_access.conditions: No visa required for Haitian nationals holding an Official or Diplomatic passport. Ordinary Haitian passport holders require an entry visa unless they hold qualifying foreign permanent residence (see other rules). Source text verbatim: 'No Visa required For Official & Diplomatic or regular Passport holders.' / 'Visa required for regular passport holders.' Note: the Visa Abolition Agreement date is cited but not specified in the PDF.

Haitian nationals holding an ordinary passport who have indefinite leave to remain (ILR / permanent residency) in the United Kingdom do not require a Barbados entry visa. Source text verbatim (official MFA PDF): 'No Visa required holders, permanent residents of UK, USA, Canada Schengen countries & Green card holders.'

Haitian nationals holding an ordinary passport who are permanent residents of the United States (Green Card holders) do not require a Barbados entry visa. Source text verbatim (official MFA PDF): 'No Visa required holders, permanent residents of UK, USA, Canada Schengen countries & Green card holders.'

Haitian nationals holding an ordinary passport who are permanent residents of Canada do not require a Barbados entry visa. Source text verbatim (official MFA PDF): 'No Visa required holders, permanent residents of UK, USA, Canada Schengen countries & Green card holders.'

Haitian nationals holding an ordinary passport who are permanent residents of a Schengen Area country do not require a Barbados entry visa. Source text verbatim (official MFA PDF): 'No Visa required holders, permanent residents of UK, USA, Canada Schengen countries & Green card holders.'

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 RE-VALIDATION: VERIFIED UNCHANGED — visa_free list (177 entries), conditional_access (Haiti, 5 rules), cbi (no program), visa_on_arrival/e_visa/eta (all empty), SERP programs (4 categories), Welcome Stamp (digital nomad), visa fees (USD$107 single / USD$211 multiple), SERP application fee (BDS$300) all confirmed against live official pages. No new inbound visa waivers found since May 9, 2025. Sources re-checked: foreign.gov.bb (MFA homepage and travelling-to-barbados page), immigration.gov.bb/pages/visa_requirements.aspx, immigration.gov.bb/pages/visitor.aspx, immigration.gov.bb/pages/SpecialEntryPermit.aspx, gisbarbados.gov.bb (visa waiver tag — returned 403), gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/barbados/entry-requirements. The Barbados-Liberia March 18, 2026 visa waiver agreement remains confirmed as outbound-only (Barbadians to Liberia). CORRECTION (this run): travelform.gov.bb form note updated — the official immigration.gov.bb/pages/visitor.aspx page confirms the form is NOT mandatory before departure ('It is not mandatory that you complete this Form before you leave as you can always use the kiosks'). Previous notes described it as 'mandatory' which was imprecise. Updated visa_policy notes field to clarify the form is encouraged but completable on arrival at a kiosk.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: CORRECTION to Liberia entry note. Previous validation (prior run) incorrectly stated the March 18, 2026 Barbados-Liberia Visa Waiver Agreement confirmed 'mutual visa-free travel for citizens of both countries.' Re-check of the official foreign.gov.bb announcement (https://www.foreign.gov.bb/barbados-and-liberia-sign-historic-visa-waiver-agreement-to-deepen-strategic-partnership/) confirms the March 2026 agreement eliminates visa requirements ONLY for Barbadian nationals travelling TO Liberia - it is NOT a reciprocal/mutual inbound waiver for Liberian nationals entering Barbados. Liberian nationals were already visa-free under the May 16, 2019 agreement. The Liberia entry in visa_free is correctly preserved; only the note has been corrected to remove the false 'mutual' claim.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: ADDITION to visa_policy notes. Barbados now requires ALL visitors (regardless of visa status) to complete a free mandatory online Immigration and Customs Form at travelform.gov.bb, available 72 hours before arrival (also completable at an on-arrival kiosk). Confirmed via immigration.gov.bb/pages/visitor.aspx and UK GOV.UK travel advice. This is an administrative form, NOT an ETA/e-visa/visa_on_arrival - visa_policy classification unchanged.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: VERIFIED UNCHANGED - visa fees at USD$107 (single) and USD$211 (multiple) confirmed against live immigration.gov.bb/pages/visa_requirements.aspx. A web search result showed BDS$200/BDS$400 figures but the official immigration.gov.bb page states USD$107/USD$211.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: VERIFIED UNCHANGED - May 9, 2025 PDF (foreign.gov.bb) remains the most current official visa list. No updated PDF found. No new inbound visa waivers identified since May 2025. The only new 2025-2026 announcement is the Barbados-Liberia agreement (outbound only, March 18, 2026).

CORRECTION (prior run): fast_track Welcome Stamp source_official changed from false to true. The official government page at foreign.gov.bb (https://www.foreign.gov.bb/side-post/barbados-welcome-stamp-programme/) confirms the program. official_url updated to point to foreign.gov.bb instead of visitbarbados.org.
CORRECTION (prior run): fast_track Welcome Stamp official_url updated from visitbarbados.org to foreign.gov.bb government page, which is the authoritative source.

CORRECTION (prior run): fast_track Welcome Stamp notes updated to include Cabinet renewal confirmation (October 26, 2023 meeting approved renewal until December 31, 2026) and clarification that participants may not offer services to Barbados-based entities.
CORRECTION (prior run): fast_track Welcome Stamp eligibility updated to add restriction on offering services to persons/entities in Barbados (sourced from official Form C-5).

CORRECTION (prior run): SERP Category 1 notes updated to flag discrepancy between official immigration.gov.bb application fee (BDS $300) and secondary-source figures (USD $3,500 / $5,000). The BDS $300 figure is what the official government page states; higher figures could not be verified from an official source.
CORRECTION (prior run): official_domains updated to add gisbarbados.gov.bb (Government Information Service Barbados - official .gov.bb domain used for government statistics and announcements).

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: visa_free list structure and all 177 entries confirmed consistent with the May 9, 2025 official MFA PDF. No new entries confirmed added post-May 2025 in the official PDF (the March 2026 Liberia agreement is outbound-only).
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: conditional_access for Haiti (5 entries) confirmed accurate per official MFA PDF.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: cbi has_program=false confirmed - no CBI program found on official sources.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: visa_on_arrival=[], e_visa=[], eta=[] confirmed - Barbados does not operate an e-visa or ETA system for incoming visitors; all non-visa-free entry is via pre-obtained online visa at apps.immigration.gov.bb.
gaps: Max stay days not explicitly stated in the PDF for several visa-free countries (shown as null); the document lists 'no visa required' without specifying duration for those entries.

SERP investment amounts (USD $2M for Cat 1, USD $300K for Cat 2) are sourced from widely-cited secondary sources; the official immigration.gov.bb SERP page only states a BDS $300 application fee and does not display investment thresholds directly. Dollar amounts are consistent across multiple secondary sources. The higher application fees cited by secondary sources (USD $3,500 under 60; USD $5,000 over 60) could not be verified from an official government URL.

Barbados Welcome Stamp official application domain barbadoswelcomestamp.bb redirects; however, program is officially documented at foreign.gov.bb and the application Form C-5 is active at barbadoswelcomestamp.bb/applynow.
No e-visa or ETA system exists for incoming visitors to Barbados - all entry is either visa-free or requires a pre-obtained online entry visa via apps.immigration.gov.bb.
Bosnia Herzegovina nationals require a visa to enter Barbados (confirmed in May 2025 PDF) - not included in visa_free list.
Dominican Republic nationals require a visa to enter Barbados (confirmed in May 2025 PDF) - not included in visa_free list.
Path to citizenship years for SERP holders not explicitly stated on official immigration page; secondary sources suggest 5 years of residency leads to eligibility for citizenship application.
Conditional access: max_stay_days for Haiti credential-based and passport-type exemptions not specified in the official MFA PDF - duration is left as null.

Conditional access: No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder rule found in any official Barbados government source - India nationals already enjoy unconditional visa-free entry (Visa Waiver Agreement May 16, 2019), making an OCI-specific rule redundant.

Conditional access: No general credential-based rule found for non-Haitian nationals (e.g., a US visa holder of any nationality getting visa-free Barbados access) - the official MFA PDF only states the PR exemption for Haitian ordinary passport holders.

Conditional access: immigration.gov.bb states 'All members of the Diplomatic Corps (of countries that require a visa) are exempt from payment of the visa fee' but they must still complete Form J - this is a fee waiver, not a visa-free entry rule.

Conditional access: The exact date of the Haiti Visa Abolition Agreement is cited in the official PDF but the date text appears cut off/illegible in the PDF rendering - could not confirm the agreement date.

The March 18, 2026 Barbados-Liberia Visa Waiver Agreement is outbound-only (Barbadians to Liberia); the Liberia entry in visa_free remains correct (already visa-free under May 2019 agreement). The prior note incorrectly described this as 'mutual' - corrected in the 2026-06-27 validation run.

travelform.gov.bb online immigration/customs form is encouraged for ALL visitors but is NOT mandatory before departure — can be completed at arrival kiosks (confirmed immigration.gov.bb/pages/visitor.aspx June 2026). The form is free and has not been classified as an ETA or e-visa as it is an administrative arrival form, not a prior travel authorization. Monitor for any future change where the form becomes a chargeable pre-clearance requirement.
No updated visa list PDF has been published since May 9, 2025. The official foreign.gov.bb website and search results confirm the May 2025 PDF remains current as of June 2026.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Barbados Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Foreign Trade PDF titled 'VISA REQUIREMENTS FOR BARBADIAN NATIONALS TRAVELLING ABROAD', Consular Section, Revised May 09, 2025. The outbound visa_free list reflects countries where Barbadian ordinary-passport holders may enter without obtaining a prior consular visa. Indonesia is visa-free for tourism/official duties but requires an e-Visa on Arrival (e-VOA) for transit. Madagascar requires no visa for stays of 15 days or less (EUR10 administrative fee on arrival); stays over 15 days require an eVisa. The Netherlands entry notes that ETIAS (not yet mandatory at document date) will be required. Countries where Barbadian nationals require a prior consular visa include: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Angola, Armenia (prior visa + visa on arrival option), Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Benin, Bhutan, Bolivia, Burundi (visa on arrival only), Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros (visa on arrival), Congo Republic, Congo DRC, Cook Islands (after 30 days), Cote d'Ivoire (online), Egypt, Eritrea, Gabon, Guinea-Bissau, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Marshall Islands, Mauritania, Micronesia, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar, Namibia, Nauru, Nepal, Niger, Nigeria, North Korea, Pakistan, Palau, Papua New Guinea, Paraguay (national passport), Qatar (ordinary passport), Rwanda, San Marino, Sao Tome & Principe, Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Tajikistan, Togo, Tonga, Ukraine, Yemen. Haiti has conditional access. Saudi Arabia has a Visa Abolition Agreement (May 16, 2019) but visa is still required per the PDF instructions. Qatar has a Visa Abolition Agreement (May 16, 2019) but ordinary passport holders need the Haya e-Visa platform.

visa_types.name: Barbados Entry Visa – Single Entry
visa_types.purpose: Permits one entry into Barbados for tourism, visiting family, or other short-stay purposes. Required only for nationals of countries not covered by a visa waiver or abolition agreement.
visa_types.notes: Single entry allows one visit within a three-month period from date of issue. Application is fully online via apps.immigration.gov.bb. Applicants submit Form J with supporting documents (passport copy, photograph, cover letter, employment/school letter, proof of funds, banker's draft payment). An entry visa is a pre-requisite but does not guarantee entry - immigration officers make final determination at the port of entry. Diplomatic corps members (whose countries require a visa) are exempt from payment of the fee but must still complete Form J.

visa_types.name: Barbados Entry Visa – Multiple Entry
visa_types.purpose: Permits multiple entries into Barbados within a six-month validity period for tourism, business visits, or family purposes.
visa_types.notes: Multiple entry visa permits numerous entries within a six-month period from date of issue. Application is fully online via apps.immigration.gov.bb (formerly paper Form J). Processing takes approximately three weeks. Cruise ship passengers in transit or arriving/departing on cruise vessels, crew members transiting within 12 hours, and direct transit passengers staying under 12 hours may be exempt from standard visa requirements.

visa_types.name: Barbados Entry Visa (Business Purpose)
visa_types.purpose: Permits entry into Barbados for business meetings, conferences, or other commercial activities. Barbados does not issue a separate business visa category - the same single or multiple entry visa is used with business supporting documentation.
visa_types.notes: Barbados does not operate a distinct business visa category. Applicants visiting for business purposes apply for the standard multiple-entry visa and must include a business-purpose cover letter and, if applicable, an employment letter from their sponsoring company. The visa category (single or multiple entry) is selected based on travel needs.

visa_types.name: Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorises foreign nationals to undertake paid employment in Barbados for a specified employer and occupation.
visa_types.notes: Work permits in Barbados are employer-sponsored and occupation-specific. Applications are submitted via the Immigration Department. SERP Category 1 holders (investors of at least USD $2 million) are also entitled to a work permit. Specific fees and processing timelines are managed through the immigration portal. Foreign nationals wishing to work in Barbados typically need both an entry visa (if their nationality requires one) and a work permit issued by the Chief Immigration Officer.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to reside in Barbados for the purpose of full-time study at a recognised educational institution.
visa_types.notes: Student visas are listed as a distinct category on the Barbados Immigration Department's services page. Applicants must provide a letter of enrollment from the educational institution. Entry visa applicants studying in Barbados must submit their school enrollment letter with their Form J application. Specific fee schedule is managed through the immigration portal at apps.immigration.gov.bb.

visa_types.name: Barbados Welcome Stamp
visa_types.purpose: Permits remote workers, freelancers, and location-independent entrepreneurs to live and work in Barbados for up to 12 months while employed by or serving clients outside Barbados.
visa_types.notes: Introduced June 30, 2020 under the Immigration Act (Form C-5). Renewable for additional 12-month periods. Individual fee: USD $2,000; Family fee: USD $3,000 (non-refundable, due within 28 days of approval). Minimum annual income requirement: USD $50,000. Participants may not accept employment from or offer services to persons or entities conducting business in Barbados. Welcome Stamp holders are not subject to Barbados income tax during the 12-month period (17.5% VAT on goods and services still applies). Cabinet approved renewal of the programme until December 31, 2026 at its meeting of October 26, 2023. Application via barbadoswelcomestamp.bb/applynow. Processing approximately 7 business days.

visa_types.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) – Category 1 (Investor)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residence and work authorisation to foreign nationals who invest at least USD $2 million in Barbados from funds sourced outside the country.
visa_types.notes: Requires investment of at least USD $2 million sourced from outside Barbados, plus demonstrated net worth exceeding USD $5 million. Category 1 SERP holders are entitled to a work permit. Official immigration.gov.bb lists a BDS $300 application fee; secondary sources cite USD $3,500 (under 60) and USD $5,000 (over 60) but these could not be confirmed from the official government page.

visa_types.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) – Category 2 (Real Estate)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residence rights to foreign nationals who own property in Barbados valued at a minimum of USD $300,000.
visa_types.notes: Requires ownership of Barbados property valued at minimum USD $300,000. Renewable 5-year permit. Category 2 SERP holders are not eligible for a work permit. Official immigration.gov.bb lists application fee of BDS $300.

visa_types.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) – Category 3 (Special Skills)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residence rights to foreign nationals who possess special skills deemed critical to the growth and development of Barbados.
visa_types.notes: No investment threshold required. Eligibility determined by the Chief Immigration Officer based on assessment of the applicant's skills and their value to Barbados's development. No specific fee, processing time or validity period was available from the official immigration.gov.bb page.

visa_types.name: Special Entry and Residence Permit (SERP) – Category 4 (Parents/Grandparents of Citizens)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residence rights to parents or grandparents of Barbadian citizens who are over 60 years of age.
visa_types.notes: Available to parents or grandparents of Barbadian citizens who are aged 60 or over. No investment threshold. Applicant must demonstrate the family relationship to a Barbadian citizen. No specific fee or processing timeline was available from the official immigration.gov.bb page.

visa_types.name: Transit Exemption (Cruise/Air)
visa_types.purpose: Exempts certain transit passengers from requiring a Barbados entry visa when transiting the country for less than 12 hours or arriving as cruise passengers in transit.
visa_types.notes: The following categories are exempt from the standard entry visa requirement: (1) cruise ship passengers in transit or arriving/departing on vessels; (2) crew members transiting within 12 hours; (3) direct transit passengers staying under 12 hours. Diplomatic corps members whose countries require a visa are exempt from the visa fee but must still complete Form J. These exemptions are stated on the official Barbados Immigration Department visa requirements page.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Passport Entry
visa_types.purpose: Allows holders of diplomatic or official passports to enter Barbados with reduced formalities, including fee waivers and, in some cases, full visa exemptions regardless of nationality.
visa_types.notes: All members of the Diplomatic Corps (of countries that require a visa to enter Barbados) are exempt from payment of the entry visa fee, but must still complete Form J. Some nationalities are visa-free for diplomatic/official passport holders but not for ordinary passport holders (e.g., Haiti: no visa required for Official and Diplomatic passport holders, but visa required for regular passport holders). The full diplomatic-passport nationality list is detailed in the official MFA PDF at foreign.gov.bb.
